content.claim: Early parental-education composition data (circa 2000) shows stark gaps: 43.6% of ninth-graders in 6/8-grade programs had mothers with tertiary education versus 21.3% in the full sample and 9.2% in vocational schools.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Data is from early 2000s; current composition may differ
Does not establish whether composition gap reflects ability selection, parental preparation investment, or other factors
Snapshot data does not track individual causal pathways into tracks
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
